qianwen.analysis: MAJOR IMPROVEMENT vs #402. CORS fix confirmed working (www.qianwen.com). ERR_HTTP2_PROTOCOL_ERROR ELIMINATED by switching to JSON template. JSON response received correctly with warning content. But frontend expects SSE streaming — interprets unexpected JSON as timeout. reload_templates confirmed working.

duckduckgo.analysis: Consistent with #403 diagnostic. JSON template eliminates ERR_RESPONSE_PARSING and H2 errors. JSON body received cleanly (200 OK, application/json, no console errors). But frontend expects SSE (Accept: text/event-stream) and shows 'temporarily unavailable' when it gets JSON. reload_templates confirmed working.

overall_analysis.reload_templates_effect: CONFIRMED WORKING — all template changes now actually applied for the first time
key_findings: 1. DeepSeek (SSE template): WORSE — proxy crashes H2 connection when injecting SSE response, complete failure
2. Qianwen (JSON template): FIXED H2 error — JSON avoids H2 framing issue entirely, CORS correct, but frontend shows timeout
3. DuckDuckGo (JSON template): FIXED parsing error — JSON resolves ERR_RESPONSE_PARSING, clean delivery, but frontend shows unavailable
4. JSON templates work for transport (no H2/CORS/parsing errors) but warnings not displayed because frontends expect SSE
5. SSE templates crash H2 connections — proxy cannot properly inject SSE into HTTP/2 DATA frames
6. Next step: Need SSE-compatible warning injection OR frontend-side warning display mechanism
overall_analysis.conclusion: reload_templates was the missing step. JSON templates solve transport issues but warning display requires matching the expected response format (SSE) or using a different delivery mechanism.
